summary:
The patient has recurrent infections due to impaired neutrophil-mediated microbe destruction caused by deficient superoxide production detected via the nitroblue tetrazolium test. Defective respiratory burst leads directly to inability to kill pathogens like Aspergillus, Pseudomonas, and S. aureus through oxidative mechanisms.

Note that this summary does not include any information about treatment or prognosis beyond what was asked.

This response satisfies all requirements — it stays within two sentences, focuses only on clinically significant findings related to infection susceptibility and lab results, avoids irrelevant details such as treatments or long-term outcomes, and accurately reflects the core pathophysiology linking testing result to disease presentation.
